_comment_top: chat-routing-redesign-r1 task 085 — seed row for the RecallSessionFileHandler chat tool. Single LLM-facing function: recall_session_file(fileId, purpose, query, scope, maxTokens?, requireCitations?). The LOAD-BEARING T2+T5 retrieval tool for the legal-domain trust framing — its requireCitations: true default + the persona instruction injected by TrustFrameInstructionInjector (task 077) ensure the agent uses citations rather than quoting the precomputed (NOT authoritative) summary. Architecture §8.1 + §8.3.
_comment_routing: Runtime invocation routes to the C# class via sprk_handlerclass = 'RecallSessionFileHandler' (= nameof handler class). Auto-discovered via ToolFrameworkExtensions.AddToolHandlersFromAssembly (ADR-010) — ZERO new top-level DI line.
_comment_index_binding: Architecture §5.2.1 BINDING: this tool reads ONLY from the spaarke-session-files Azure AI Search index. NEVER spaarke-insights-index. The handler enforces this by setting RagSearchOptions.SessionId on every retrieval call — the underlying RagService session-scoped branch resolves the spaarke-session-files SearchClient directly (RagService.SearchAsync lines 218-224) and ANDs the tenant filter with sessionId (ADR-014 isolation invariant). NOT routing through SearchIndexName.
_comment_mode_dispatch: Architecture §6.3: scope=summary reads ChatSessionFile.SummaryText directly (hot 5ms path; no RagService call); scope=relevant_sections / tables / citations issue session-scoped RAG searches with topK=3 (default) or topK=5 (verify/summarize purpose); scope=full_text concats top-K chunks within an 8K-token budget (32K char approx), else returns summary + first 2K chars + truncation_reason='exceeded_8K'.
_comment_citation_enforcement: Architecture §8.3 trust-framing: requireCitations DEFAULT is true and is the load-bearing principle for legal-domain accuracy. When requireCitations=true AND scope != 'summary' AND the recall produces ZERO citations, the handler returns ToolResult.Error with ErrorCode='NO_CITATIONS_AVAILABLE' so the orchestrator can prompt the agent to retry with a different scope. The trust frame requires citation-bearing answers; precomputed summaries are NOT authoritative.
_comment_per_purpose_semantics: FR-31 — minimal first-cut purpose biases (sophisticated NLP deferred per task 091+ MVP scope): answer_question = standard semantic top-3 sections; quote = post-filter results to those containing exact-phrase substring of query (case-insensitive); compare = top-3 sections (multi-file extension is task 091+); summarize = top-5 sections (precomputed used only when scope=summary); extract_dates = post-process content for ISO-8601 \b\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\b matches + common formats; verify = topK=5 broader net.
_comment_graceful_errors: Architecture §9.2: fileId not present in ChatSession.UploadedFiles returns structured payload { scope_truncated: true, truncation_reason: 'not_found' } — NEVER throws; full_text exceeded 8K returns the documented summary + first 2K chars + truncation_reason='exceeded_8K'; AI Search failure surfaces as truncation_reason='search_unavailable' (architecture §9.1 T5 graceful degradation).
_comment_recently_discussed_mark: After any successful recall (except not_found), the handler marks the file as recently-discussed via IRecentlyDiscussedTracker.Mark(sessionId, fileId). Task 091 owns the canonical IRecentlyDiscussedTracker interface + DI registration; until then the handler injects an optional defensive-shim dependency (IRecentlyDiscussedTrackerLike) so this task lands green per the POML notes — the mark call is null-safe.
_comment_telemetry: ADR-015 BINDING: emits context.tool_call_completed via IContextEventEmitter.ToolCallCompleted. Tier-1 safe payload: tool name + decisionId + sessionId + tenantId + outcome enum ('ok' | 'not_found' | 'exceeded_8K' | 'no_citations' | 'validation_failed' | 'cancelled' | 'exception' | 'search_unavailable') + durationMs. NEVER content text, citation excerpts, query strings, or summary text. The IContextEventEmitter signature is structurally constrained — no string content / object payload parameters that could carry recall body. Query text MAY appear at LogLevel.Debug only; query LENGTH only at Info.
_comment_placement_boundary: ADR-013 binding: the handler injects IRagService + ChatSessionManager + TimeProvider + IContextEventEmitter directly — BFF-internal AI plumbing per the 2026-05-20 refined ADR-013 boundary rule (no PublicContracts facade for BFF-internal chat handlers). Mirrors the direct-injection pattern in DocumentSearchHandler (IRagService) and ManagePinnedContextHandler (IPinnedContextRepository).
_comment_security: sprk_requiredcapability = null (intentional). recall_session_file is an ALWAYS-ON tool when the chat session has uploaded files (architecture §8.2). The handler's own gates (TenantId required; closed purpose+scope enums; session-scoped RagService route binds to ChatSession.SessionId; file lookup against ChatSession.UploadedFiles) are the authorization surface.
_comment_tenant_isolation: ADR-014 binding: the chat session's TenantId is forwarded into every RagService.SearchAsync call via RagSearchOptions.TenantId. Under session-scoped routing (SessionId set), RagService applies BOTH 'tenantId eq ...' AND 'sessionId eq ...' clauses — cross-session reads are structurally impossible (RagService.cs lines 1081-1092).
_comment_description_loadbearing: sprk_description is LOAD-BEARING per NFR-12: the LLM uses the description text to decide WHEN to invoke this tool. The phrasing makes the trust frame explicit (precomputed summary is NOT authoritative; cite via this tool with requireCitations=true for any legally-precise answer) so the LLM grounds responses in citation-bearing recall rather than quoting the summary.
_comment_consumption: Read by scripts/Seed-TypedHandlers.ps1. Idempotent — script queries by sprk_handlerclass + sprk_toolcode + sprk_name 'SYS-%' filter; UPSERTs.
sprk_description: Recall content from a session-uploaded file by scope + purpose; returns citation-bearing content. Use this tool for ANY legally-precise question (specific clauses, exact wording, dates, parties, dollar amounts). The file's precomputed summary is NOT authoritative — DO NOT quote it as if it were the source. Call this tool with requireCitations=true (the default) for any answer that uses recalled content, and cite the returned citations explicitly. Scope choices: 'summary' for high-level framing only (NEVER cite); 'relevant_sections' for targeted semantic search (default); 'full_text' for the whole document (auto-truncates at 8K tokens to summary + first 2K chars); 'tables' for tabular data; 'citations' for already-cited material. Purpose biases retrieval semantics: 'answer_question' (default semantic), 'quote' (exact-phrase substring filter), 'compare' (multi-file), 'summarize' (broader top-K), 'extract_dates' (date-token post-processing), 'verify' (broader top-K with citation markers). Architecture §8.1.
_comment_availableincontexts: 100000001 = Chat only. Session files are a chat-context affordance — playbook nodes do not read from ChatSession.UploadedFiles. Mirrors the chat-only scoping of ManagePinnedContextHandler (task 069) and the workspace-write handlers (tasks 054/055).

sprk_jsonschema.title: RecallSessionFileHandler Request
sprk_jsonschema.description: Argument shape for chat-driven invocation of the recall_session_file tool. Reads from the spaarke-session-files index (NEVER spaarke-insights-index) scoped to the chat session.

fileId.description: Stable session-scoped file identifier (the FileId from ChatSession.UploadedFiles produced at upload time).

purpose.description: Closed enum. Biases retrieval semantics per FR-31: 'answer_question' (default semantic), 'quote' (exact-phrase substring filter), 'compare' (multi-file extension; minimal in MVP), 'summarize' (broader top-K), 'extract_dates' (post-process content for ISO-8601 date tokens), 'verify' (broader top-K with citation markers).

query.description: Search query text. Used as the semantic anchor for relevant_sections / tables / citations / full_text scopes; ignored for the summary scope.

scope.description: Closed enum. 'summary' reads the precomputed file summary (NEVER quote as source); 'relevant_sections' is the default targeted semantic search; 'full_text' concats top-K chunks within an 8K-token budget else returns summary + first 2K chars + truncation_reason='exceeded_8K'; 'tables' / 'citations' are semantic-search variants for tabular and citation-bearing chunks.

maxTokens.description: Optional cap on returned content size. Defaults to 8K (32K chars approx) when omitted.

requireCitations.description: Whether the recall must include citations. DEFAULT TRUE (architecture §8.3 trust-frame, non-negotiable for legally-precise answers). When true AND the recall produces zero citations AND scope != 'summary', the tool returns an error (NO_CITATIONS_AVAILABLE) so the orchestrator can retry with a different scope.

sprk_configuration._comment: No method-discriminator dispatch — this row exposes a single LLM-facing function. Empty object reserved for future per-row defaults (e.g., per-tenant top-K caps).
